summary: Ukrainian forces struck three key bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk, disrupting Russian logistics. Russian UAV activity continues across multiple fronts, targeting Ukrainian cities and infrastructure. Both sides report ongoing drone strikes and casualties in contested regions.
key_points: Ukrainian forces damaged an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk in Donetsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk, hindering Russian troop and supply movements. (Source: @UkraineNow/82268)
A KAB strike on Kharkiv killed a 23‑year‑old woman, injured ten people, and damaged a tram, power lines, vehicles and buildings. (Source: @UkraineNow/82267)
Russian‑controlled drones launched from the Black Sea toward Limanka in Odesa region and toward Chornomorska from the sea. (Source: @kpszsu/66813, @kpszsu/66819)
Ukrainian intelligence reported multiple reactive UAVs approaching Poltava, Sumy, Chernihiv and Kharkiv from various directions. (Source: @eRadarrua/98668, @kpszsu/66761, @kpszsu/66816)
Russian drone strikes hit energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia, causing outages across the region and in Kherson; the Crimean Bridge was closed following attacks. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
A kamikaze drone hit a bus in Petryatinka, Bryansk region, wounding a woman; an FPV drone attacked a commercial facility in Proletarsky, Belgorod region. (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237)
Russian diesel supplies are reported as abundant, with discussions of export bans to prioritize domestic market. (Source: @tass_agency/382955)
A fire at a refinery in Krasnodar Krai was caused by falling UAV debris. (Source: @tass_agency/382937)

sections.executive_summary: Ukrainian forces damaged three strategic bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk, impairing Russian logistics. Russian UAV operations persist across multiple fronts, targeting Ukrainian cities and infrastructure. Both sides continue to exchange drone strikes, resulting in civilian casualties and infrastructure damage.

points: Ukrainian strike on bridges: an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk (Donetsk) and two railway bridges in Luhansk were hit, disrupting Russian troop and supply movements (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
KAB strike on Kharkiv caused one civilian death, ten injuries, and damage to a tram, power lines, over fifteen vehicles, a factory building and nearby houses (Source: @UkraineNow/82267).
Ukrainian forces attacked a private enterprise in Dnipro, resulting in seven injured, including a 22‑year‑old in serious condition (Source: @UkraineNow/82256).
Ukrainian intelligence reported reactive UAVs heading toward Poltava (Kotelva area) from the east and toward Sumy (Trostyanets) from the northeast (Source: @eRadarrua/98668, @kpszsu/66816).
Ukrainian reports indicate UAV activity over Chernihiv region, with units moving toward Horodny from the north and toward Goryny from the south (Source: @kpszsu/66764).
Ukrainian forces destroyed a bridge over the Senek River using an FAB strike to hinder Russian logistics (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian units engaged in small‑arms battles in multiple villages of Sumy and Kharkiv directions, including Bachevsk, Pysarevka and Kazachya Lopan (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian‑controlled drones launched from the Black Sea toward Limanka in Odesa region and toward Chornomorska, indicating maritime UAV deployment (Source: @kpszsu/66813, @kpszsu/66819).
Reactive UAVs were observed moving from Donetsk toward Gulyaypole in Zaporizhzhia, suggesting cross‑border drone activity (Source: @eRadarrua/98626).
Russian diesel reserves are reported as abundant, with discussions of export bans to increase domestic supply (Source: @tass_agency/382955).
A fire at a refinery in Krasnodar Krai was caused by falling UAV debris, highlighting risks of UAV operations near critical infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/382937).
Russian forces reported using kamikaze drones against civilian targets in Bryansk region, hitting a bus in Petryatinka and wounding a woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
FPV drone attacks on commercial facilities in Proletarsky, Belgorod region caused barotrauma to a civilian (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian troops reportedly liberated Bogodarovka in Dnipropetrovsk region, according to the Russian Defense Ministry (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31243).
Ukrainian intelligence reported reactive UAVs moving toward Kharkiv from the north (near Bogoduhiv) and toward Kovyag from the south (Source: @kpszsu/66761).
Ukrainian forces noted UAV strikes on energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia, causing widespread outages in the region and in neighboring Kherson (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian drone activity was observed over Sumy direction, with assaults near Shostka district and small‑arms fighting in Bachevsk and surrounding villages (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian units reported destroying a bridge over the Senek River in the Kupyansk direction using FAB strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAV attacks on the settlement of Pysantsy in Dnipropetrovsk direction, advancing along the Vovcha River (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian forces continued assault operations in urban areas of Krasny Liman and Konstantinovka, facing Ukrainian drone strikes (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian UAV sightings included reactive drones heading toward Nikopol district from north‑west and west (Source: @kpszsu/66783).
Ukrainian intelligence reported reactive UAVs moving toward Chernihiv region: one toward Horodny from the north and another toward Goryny from the south (Source: @kpszsu/66764).
UAVs were observed heading toward Holmy in Chernihiv from the east (Source: @kpszsu/66788).
UAV activity reported near Lebedyn, Guty and Slobozhanske in Kharkiv region (Source: @eRadarrua/98654).
Reactive UAVs were noted moving toward Kotelva in Poltava from the east (Source: @eRadarrua/98668).
UAVs observed near Chervone in Poltava heading westward from Sumy direction (Source: @kpszsu/66820).
UAV sightings included a pair heading from the sea toward Ochakiv (Source: @eRadarrua/98623).
UAVs reported near Koshugum in Zaporizhzhia from the south (Source: @kpszsu/66810).
Ukrainian strike damaged bridges in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk, affecting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
Russian UAV attacks on energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia caused outages across the region and in Kherson; the Crimean Bridge was closed after attacks (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian forces reported UAV activity toward Limanka in Odesa region from the Black Sea (Source: @kpszsu/66813).
UAVs were observed heading toward Chornomorska from the Black Sea, indicating southern maritime drone operations (Source: @kpszsu/66819).
Russian drone strikes targeted a private enterprise in Dnipro, injuring seven people (Source: @UkraineNow/82256).
Ukrainian reports noted reactive UAVs moving from Donetsk toward Gulyaypole in Zaporizhzhia (Source: @eRadarrua/98626).
UAV activity reported near Kherson region with attacks on a bus depot, destroying civilian vehicles including school buses (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian air defenses shot down at least two UAVs over Crimea, prompting air raid warnings and closure of the Crimean Bridge (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Russian forces conducted intensive drone strikes on Ukrainian territory, including Kharkiv, Sumy, Pavlovgrad and Mykolaiv regions (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Kamikaze drone struck a bus in Petryatinka, Bryansk region, wounding a civilian woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
FPV drone attacked a commercial facility in Proletarsky, Belgorod region, causing barotrauma (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian reports noted multiple reactive UAVs operating over Poltava, Sumy, Chernihiv and Kharkiv, indicating widespread aerial activity (Source: @eRadarrua/98668, @kpszsu/66761).
Ukrainian forces reported shooting down UAVs over the Black Sea approaches to Odesa and Zaporizhzhia (Source: @kpszsu/66813).
Russian UAV debris caused a fire at a refinery in Krasnodar Krai, highlighting the risk of aerial incidents near critical infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/382937).

key_developments: Ukrainian forces damaged an automobile bridge near Novoazovsk and two railway bridges in Luhansk, disrupting Russian logistics (Source: @UkraineNow/82268).
A KAB strike on Kharkiv killed a civilian and injured ten, damaging transport and power infrastructure (Source: @UkraineNow/82267).
Russian drones launched from the Black Sea toward Limanka (Odesa) and Chornomorska, indicating maritime UAV operations (Source: @kpszsu/66813, @kpszsu/66819).
Energy facilities in Zaporizhzhia were hit, causing regional power outages and leading to the closure of the Crimean Bridge (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Ukrainian intelligence reported reactive UAVs moving toward Poltava, Sumy, Chernihiv and Kharkiv from multiple directions (Source: @eRadarrua/98668, @kpszsu/66761).
A kamikaze drone struck a bus in Petryatinka, Bryansk region, wounding a woman (Source: @ukr_leaks_eng/31237).
Fire at a Krasnodar refinery was caused by falling UAV debris, highlighting UAV risks to critical infrastructure (Source: @tass_agency/382937).
sections.threat_assessment: Ukrainian bridge attacks are degrading Russian supply lines in occupied Donetsk and Luhansk, potentially limiting force mobility. Russian UAV deployments from the Black Sea and inland are targeting critical infrastructure in southern Ukraine, sustaining pressure on energy and transport networks. The proliferation of reactive UAVs across northern and eastern fronts suggests increased low‑altitude aerial activity, complicating air defense and ground operations for both sides.
sections.regional_response: Ukrainian command appears to prioritize high‑value infrastructure strikes, leveraging precision munitions to disrupt Russian logistics. Russian authorities are reinforcing fuel supplies domestically while continuing UAV strikes, indicating a focus on sustaining frontline operations despite logistical challenges. Neighboring regions report collateral damage from UAV debris, prompting heightened civil defense measures.
sections.intelligence_notes: UAV sightings are reported from multiple directions, often specifying origin and target corridors, which can aid in mapping enemy drone launch sites. The closure of the Crimean Bridge after attacks may affect Russian supply routes to Crimea. Continued civilian casualties from drone strikes underscore the need for improved air defense coverage, especially in densely populated areas.
